title: The Moon and 3I
wikitext:
[]: The Moon and 3I             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Mon May 05, 2008 11:57 pm Alright, by hobby, I am an amateur astronomer. As I was gazing at the stars this evening, I thought, 'Wouldn't it be possible to figure out the date the beach scene happened using astronomy?" So began a mini-quest of sorts to unravel the date of the beach scene. Now, as this is an anime, with the night sky not the main focus of the scene, my results can be only haphazard. I can make an educated guess as to the date, and even the latitude of the beach. http://i302.photobucket.com/albums/nn111/KR_Chos/Combinedview.jpg This image here pretty much contains most of the information I need to make my educated guess. Here, the moon is full, and sits perched on the eastern side of a swath of Milky Way. The only two times a band of Milky Way would play host to a full moon is when that band sits opposite to the sun. Now, the Milky Way is crossed by the sun in two different places, which place would it be? In winter, the sun sits opposite a faint, continuous band of Milky Way, and in summer, it sits opposite a bright, *split* band of Milky Way. This image here, shows that the Summer Milky Way is beginning to slide down towards the west, with the open, split end heading south. Based on the split, it would appear that the constellation of Cygnus would have passed overhead for that location, which happens at mid-northern latitudes. So I can conclude that Earth's axis wasn't moved around much, if at all, by Third Impact. Using this information, it is a simple task to find the date where the scene takes place. All that has to be done is find a date where the full moon is leaving the eastern edge of the summer Milky Way when it is full. I used a moon phase calculator to find the dates of full moons when it was near the Milky Way. I got dates of May 21, June 20, July 19, and August 18. I quickly ruled May 21 out, as the full moon was placed on the opposite side of the Milky Way. August 18 was out too, it was too far removed from the band of the Milky Way on the eastern side. That left June 20 and July 19 plausible dates, with July 19 providing the best fit to the date. A sky map for the date: So, I guess this is something interesting that comes about when one hobby gets mixed with another. TL;DR: I figured out that the beach scene in End of Evangelion takes place July 19, 2016. Go figure. Another odd little tidbit I dug up while "researching" this article, is that the moon is in a crescent phase when the world ends, yet it is full when Asuka comes back. That means *at least* 14 days between 3I and her return.

[]: Re: The Moon and 3I             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue May 06, 2008 12:03 am CorporalChaos wrote: Another odd little tidbit I dug up while "researching" this article, is that the moon is in a crescent phase when the world ends, yet it is full when Asuka comes back. That means *at least* 14 days between 3I and her return.

[]: Re: The Moon and 3I             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue May 06, 2008 12:23 am Sachi_13 wrote: Doesn't explain the rusted cross necklace.

[]: Re: The Moon and 3I             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue May 06, 2008 12:24 am Sachi_13 wrote: 14 days alone would be enough to send Shinji crazy enough to choke Asuka. Doesn't explain the rusted cross necklace.

[]: Re: The Moon and 3I             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue May 06, 2008 12:28 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Going through 3I and the rest of that shit was enough to make Shinji crazy enough to choke Asuka. 14 days alone on a seemingly void Earth was just the icing on the nutty cake.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue May 06, 2008 12:31 am Think about it, 14 days to think he'd killed everyone... Guilt trip starts there then all the other crap comes back in force.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue May 06, 2008 1:04 am And for all the people who take 3I as happening on 1-Jan-16, then there's been more than enough time for Shinji to lose his fingernail grasp on reality.

[]: Re: The Moon and 3I             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue May 06, 2008 2:48 am Sachi_13 wrote: He was supposedly " alright " when he rejected Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue May 06, 2008 2:53 am Great analysis. Quite right, Tines, there's plenty of time here for Shinji (Or for that matter, any sane person) to go completely bonkers. And the rust on that cross came from the nail, not the cross itself!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue May 06, 2008 7:08 pm CC wrote: TL;DR: I figured out that the beach scene in End of Evangelion takes place July 19, 2016. Go figure.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue May 06, 2008 7:35 pm Great analysis! I never thought that you could go quite so in depth with such a detail. I agree with the idea that salt water (cough LCL cough) and changes in climate could easily have resulted in the rust stain. That and its possible the nail was rusty to begin with, which would have speeded up things considerably.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue May 06, 2008 8:05 pm I doubt much thought was put into the Milky Way's relation to the moon (if thought were put into that sort of thing, we would see actual constellations in the sky instead of randomly placed stars. I would, however, say that the phases of the moon are important in showing how much time has passed. To SSD: He's not YOUR Shinji-kun; he belongs to the world! >=(

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Tue May 06, 2008 8:37 pm NAveryW wrote: I doubt much thought was put into the Milky Way's relation to the moon (if thought were put into that sort of thing, we would see actual constellations in the sky instead of randomly placed stars. I would, however, say that the phases of the moon are important in showing how much time has passed.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue May 06, 2008 8:39 pm You know they could've just been too lazy to go into such fine detail as the constellations. That'd be an insane amount of detail. As long as it's identified as the Milky Way fine detail isn't that important. It could go either way. So we're left with a minimal of 14 days to a max of 8 months... I'd so love some explaination for that crap. The Bullshit-o-meter can't even rate the sheer stupidity Anno has thrown our way this time. The flipping car was bad enough but this ...

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Tue May 06, 2008 8:51 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: You know they could've just been too lazy to go into such fine detail as the constellations. That'd be an insane amount of detail. As long as it's identified as the Milky Way fine detail isn't that important. It could go either way. So we're left with a minimal of 14 days to a max of 8 months... I'd so love some explaination for that crap. The Bullshit-o-meter can't even rate the sheer stupidity Anno has thrown our way this time. The flipping car was bad enough but this ...

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue May 06, 2008 9:11 pm Either way it's still Anno brushing off real world physics when it suits him. He's really good at it even for things that he really shouldn't do it for.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue May 06, 2008 9:25 pm B-b-but the Rip Van Winkle Effect! So, out of curiosity, what would be the first occurrence of the crescent moon in 2016? (Dunno if the year makes any difference. I am totally severed from Nature and never look at the night sky, or even at the daytime sky for that matter.)

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Tue May 06, 2008 9:29 pm Reichu wrote: B-b-but the Rip Van Winkle Effect! So, out of curiosity, what would be the first occurrence of the crescent moon in 2016? (Dunno if the year makes any difference. I am totally severed from Nature and never look at the night sky, or even at the daytime sky for that matter.)

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue May 06, 2008 10:02 pm Lol, that's rather funny. Paid enough attention to what the moon phase should be, just not where the moon itself should be.
